channel.title: Sir Abdisalam | The Website
channel.description: Recent content on Sir Abdisalam | The Website

item.title: Random Thought On The Brothers Karamazov
item.description:
Preview

  • Intro : My current thoughts on Book 1

Intro : My current thoughts on Book 1

I’m currently reading the book, and I must say, it begins at a rather slow pace though this is, of course, typical of Fyodor’s style. Books One was excellent, revealing a deeply dysfunctional family, which I believe is entirely the result of Fyodor Pavlovich’s character.

item.title: I Built Inbox Subscription Cleaner Because My Gmail Was Cooked
item.description:
(hint: click on the image to zoom in)

I have had the same Gmail since 2017. Back then it was clean, fresh, full of potential. Fast forward to now and it looked like a digital flea market. Every brand I ever looked at once was suddenly my pen pal. My inbox was not an inbox anymore. It was a subscription museum.

I tried the normal “just unsubscribe” advice. Sounds good until you do it for the 47th time in a row. Open email. Scroll to the footer like you are searching for treasure. Click tiny link. Land on a page that says, “Are you sure you want to leave our family?” Yes. I am sure. Then repeat. At some point I realized this was not productivity. This was unpaid labor.

item.title: Desire and Its Nature Pt 3 : Discipline
item.description:
"The soul in its early stages is like a rebellious horse. If not trained, it will never obey you."
— Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya

I think the more I reflect on this series, the more I realize how little I actually knew about desire when I first started writing it all. It seemed like something simple at the start. A feeling really only. Something that could either be ignored or acted on. But now, after sitting with it, questioning , if i really wanted this, falling to it sometimes and trying to rise again. The act of wanting, and that of wanting new things, which I mentioned in the last part, is what desire is; if not controlled with discipline, NOT pure motivation.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
Inbox Subscription Cleaner - February 2026

My Gmail was a mess of subscriptions, so I built a tool to fix it. It scans for newsletters, groups them by sender, and lets you unsubscibe in bulk. It handles both one-click unsubscribes and manual ones efficiently.

Read about how I built it

Game Theory Simulation - April 6th 2025

After searching for a game theory simulation that wasn’t just educational but actually allowed competitive play, I couldn’t find one that fit. Most were limited to static demos or textbook-style visuals. So, I started building my own version of the classic Prisoner’s Dilemma as a side project. This is version 1, with support for both multiplayer over a local network and singleplayer versus a bot. I plan to improve it over time, but I’m publishing this first iteration on GitHub soon as a reference for anyone interested.

item.title: Desire and Its Nature – Pt. 2
item.description:
"Man is broad, too broad. I'd have him narrower."
— Fyodor Dostoevsky

This is a continuation of my earlier thoughts on desire. I still don’t have clear answers, but I am starting to see the layers more clearly. The surface is really obvious. Beneath it, things get harder and harder to define.

What I’ve been noticing lately is how quickly desire changes. The moment one goes out, another takes its place. It is not about what is wanted, but about the state of wanting itself ( which I think is insane to think of ). That condition seems to persist, regardless of whether anything is actually missing at all.

item.title: Desire and it's Nature
item.description:
You can listen to my thoughts!
0:00
--:--
"The chains of habit are too weak to be felt until they are too strong to be broken."
— Samuel Johnson

Recently, I have been thinking if my desires-good or bad-control me? Desire. It really is one of the greatest walls standing between a man and his own peace. Not all desires are evil, but the moment they become the master, the soul begins to rot. I have come to see more recently, there is something deceptive about it, the way it starts as a whisper(gentle), almost harmless. A itching for ease, for pleasure, for something just out of reach. It does not force itself upon you; it only asks. Wouldn’t it be easier? Wouldn’t it be nice? What harm is there in just this once? Or What if I just…

item.title: My 2025 Checklist
item.description:
This is the list I have laid out for myself, though I expect new ideas will arise as January unfolds. It serves as a rough outline, a gathering of thoughts and aspirations for the year ahead. My hope is that, by this time next year, these goals will stand fulfilled, each box checked with purpose.

X
Be conversational in Turkish
Build some Swift apps
X
Land new career opportunities
X
Read 110 Books
X
Bring new branches into The SISU Foundation
X
Revise the Quran
X
Get into cooking
X
Bring out the prototype of Journa Media
Master Web 2.0
Travel to Liechtenstein
X
Try to get 3.7-4.0 GPA this year
?
Do a Marathon run **

Planning to run the Istanbul Marathon in April 2025. Currently training and preparing for this goal.

item.title: 2024 Reflection
item.description:
Listen to my thoughts
0:00
--:--

2024 . What a year it was. The start of the year was epic, I had so much motivation to go do it all but as the year went by that motivation went away slowly. Which is why the quote

“Thinking is easy, acting is difficult, and to put one’s thoughts into action is the most difficult thing in the world.” – Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Abdisalam Sharif Ali

Well… Welcome! I’m a lover of books-classical ones mostly-a runner at heart, and someone who’s never shy about speaking my mind. If there’s one thing you should know about me, it’s that I believe in standing up for what I think is right-and doing something about it.

I am in love with trying to bring education everywhere (pretty crazy that isn’t the case). I’m also a huge fan of the journalism industry and believe that traditional journalism is dying at the expense of money and greed (but I’ll probably go on and on about this in a blog post later).

item.title: My Books
item.description:
Gold or blue links: I wrote a longer post. **: a short note. Still filling this in.

Fiction

Novels and stories read for plot, voice, and worlds.

  1. The Vanising Half - Brit Bennett

  2. Love from A to Z - S.K Ali **

    I read this back when I was about 14, it was a good read at the time!!
